name: Zuhayr ibn Qayn
title: Commander of the Right Wing
shortDescription: A prominent man from the Bajila tribe who, after a profound spiritual transformation, became one of the Imam's most loyal commanders.
fullStory:
Zuhayr ibn Qayn al-Bajali was a brave elder and prominent figure of Kufa. Initially, he had Uthmani leanings and avoided meeting Imam Hussain (AS). On the route from Mecca to Kufa, Zuhayr's caravan always tried to encamp far from the Imam's caravan. However, at one stopping place (Zarud), both caravans inevitably encamped at the same location.

Imam Hussain (AS) sent a messenger to Zuhayr's tent, inviting him for a meeting. Zuhayr initially hesitated, but encouraged by his brave wife (Dalham bint Amr), he went to the Imam's tent. This meeting caused such a massive spiritual transformation in his soul that he returned with a radiant and joyful face. He moved his tent next to the Imam's tents, gave all his wealth to his wife, divorced her so she could safely return to her family, and wholeheartedly joined the Hussaini caravan.

According to the report in Sheikh Mufid's Al-Irshad, on the morning of Ashura, when Imam Hussain (AS) was arranging his small army, recognizing Zuhayr's military experience and bravery, he appointed him as the commander of the right wing (Maymana) of the army. Throughout the battle, Zuhayr constantly acted as a shield for the Imam, especially during the noon prayer of Ashura when he, along with Sa'id ibn Abdullah, stood in front of the Imam to protect him from arrows until the prayer was finished.

Sheikh Mufid writes in Al-Irshad: Zuhayr, along with Habib ibn Mazahir, launched coordinated and heroic attacks on the enemy ranks. Whenever one of them was surrounded, the other would quickly rush to his aid and rescue him. Zuhayr severely terrified the enemies with his powerful war poetry and inflicted heavy casualties on the Kufan army.

After a long and heroic battle, Kathir ibn Abdullah al-Sha'bi and Muhajir ibn Aws finally attacked and martyred him. When Zuhayr fell to the ground, Imam Hussain (AS) came to his side and said: 'O Zuhayr, may God not deprive you of His mercy and may He curse your killers like the accursed transformed ones'.
quoteArabic: وَاللَّهِ لَوَدِدْتُ أَنِّي قُتِلْتُ ثُمَّ نُشِرْتُ ثُمَّ قُتِلْتُ حَتَّى أُقْتَلَ كَذَا أَلْفَ مَرَّةٍ
quote: By God, I wish to be killed, resurrected, and killed again a thousand times in defense of you.
